diagrams.title: BGP Message Flow and State Machine
diagrams.description: Shows the BGP finite state machine with 6 states and message exchanges between two BGP peers
elements: BGP messages: OPEN, UPDATE, KEEPALIVE, NOTIFICATION
diagrams.purpose: Understand BGP neighbor establishment process and troubleshooting connection issues

diagrams.title: AS-PATH Loop Prevention Mechanism
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how BGP prevents routing loops by examining AS-PATH attribute before accepting routes
elements: Three Autonomous Systems: AS100, AS200, AS300
Arrows showing route advertisement with AS-PATH
Example showing AS100 rejecting route containing '100' in path
Path sequence visualization: AS100→AS200→AS300, then AS300→AS200→AS100
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how loop detection works and why own AS in path causes route rejection

diagrams.title: eBGP and iBGP Topology
diagrams.description: Shows physical and logical BGP connections in a typical network with multiple AS
elements: AS100 with routers R1, R2, iBGP mesh between them
eBGP connection between R1 (AS100) and R3 (AS200)
iBGP connection between R2 (AS100) and R4 (AS200)
Route reflector (optional) to reduce iBGP mesh
diagrams.purpose: Understand difference between external and internal BGP, their placement, and NEXT-HOP handling
